payload.description: Transform Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards into a complete street where buses operate quickly and reliably, bus customers safely access stations, pedestrians are comfortable, and drivers go at reasonable safe speeds

More than 60% of deliveries take 15 minutes or less

tool_args.label: 45% say customer parking is a major concern
23% say customer parking is a concern
32% say customer parking is not a concern
61% of Cross Bay vs 26% along Woodhaven offer off-street parking

payload.claim_text: 61% of Cross Bay businesses offer off-street parking compared to only 26% along Woodhaven
payload.subject: off-street parking availability

payload.claim_text: Surveyors visited over 350 businesses along the corridor in Jan/Feb 2015. Over 200 businesses filled out the survey. Asked a standard set of questions focusing on deliveries and parking. Results will help inform curb regulations and other considerations as design progresses.
payload.subject: business outreach

tool_args.label: 68% of businesses reported concern about customer parking

payload.claim_text: 68% of businesses reported concern about customer parking

payload.description: 4 workshops focusing on different corridor sections: 1. April 16 2015 - Woodhaven Blvd (Union Tpke to Rockaway Blvd) 2. April 23 2015 - Woodhaven Blvd (Queens Blvd to Union Tpke) 3. April 29 2015 - Cross Bay Blvd 4. April 30 2015 - The Rockaways

warnings: event.organizers is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organizer lists refer to entity records. Suggested relation: organized_by.

tool_args.label: Over 180 community members attended public design workshops

payload.description: Over 180 members of the community attended. Participants had opportunity to have group discussions at small tables and comment directly on the draft designs.

May 13, 2015 - CB 6 Full Board; May 15 - Queens Borough President; May 28 - Broad Channel Civic Assoc.; June 1 - CB 14 Transportation Committee; June 4 - AM Miller / SS Addabbo; June 8 - Congresswoman Meng; June 9 - CB 9 Full Board; June 17 - CB 5 Leadership Field Meeting; June 29 - Rockaway Beach Civic Assoc.; June 30 - Howard Beach - Lindenwood Civic Assoc.
Woodhaven / Cross Bay Boulevard (Q52/53) Community Advisory Committee Meeting #4

Summer 2015: Refine design plans based on community feedback and further technical review. Fall 2015: Present revised design to the community. Fall 2015: Transfer project to NYC Department of Design and Construction for Final Design and engineering.
Bus lanes will operate 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
Median bus stations with fencing to prevent unsafe crossing of the service road
Refuge islands will provide a safe place to wait and protection from turning cars for pedestrians crossing the street

tool_args.label: Left turn restrictions at major intersections

payload.description: Left turn restrictions at major intersections reduce conflicts with crossing pedestrians
Off-board fare collection with MTA Eagle Team education and enforcement; 48-80% reduction in fare evasion on SBS routes

warnings: treatment_component.enforcement_authority is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Authority names refer to agency/entity records. Suggested relation: enforced_by.

tool_args.label: Provides connections to 8 subway lines, over 20 bus routes, and the LIRR

payload.claim_text: Provides connections to 8 subway lines, over 20 bus routes, and the LIRR

tool_args.label: Left turning crashes vs right turning crashes for pedestrian injuries

payload.claim_text: A NYCDOT study found that left turning crashes resulting in serious pedestrian injuries outnumber right turning crashes 3 to 1.
payload.subject: left turn crash safety
payload.source: The New York City Pedestrian Safety Study & Action Plan, August 2010

warnings: claim.source is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Source/program names are relation context, not claim type. Suggested relation: data_provided_by or part_of_program.

tool_args.label: Eagle Team fare enforcement - 16% summons rate

payload.claim_text: The MTA's Eagle Team uses education and enforcement for fare compliance on SBS routes. The vast majority of the Eagle Team's random checks are warnings and assists - only 16% of stops result in a summons. On SBS routes, there has been a 48-80% reduction in fare evasion.

tool_args.label: 16% of Eagle Team stops result in a summons

payload.raw_value_text: only 16% of stops result in a summons
payload.scope: MTA Eagle Team random checks on SBS routes

tool_args.label: 48-80% reduction in fare evasion on SBS routes

payload.raw_value_text: there has been a 48-80% reduction in fare evasion

tool_args.label: Project will help traffic by reducing bottlenecks, improving signal timing

payload.claim_text: The project will help traffic by reducing bottlenecks, improving signal timing, and creating a consistent roadway design. During the concept screening analysis, initial results showed improved travel times.

tool_args.label: Plan accommodates all existing traffic, no diversions expected

payload.claim_text: The plan is designed to accommodate all traffic that currently uses Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards. Since all traffic will be accommodated, we do not expect diversions to parallel streets.

warnings: claim.route is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route ids should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.
